title: About Tueely
summary: Overview of Tueely as a restaurant AI platform, its products MenuQR and MenuGPT, its mission to elevate restaurant menus with AI, and who it is built for.

children.title: Company Overview
children.summary: What Tueely is, its mission, and the problem it solves for restaurants.

children.title: What Is Tueely
children.summary: Tueely is a restaurant AI platform that augments physical in-house menus with AI to accurately answer food-related queries.
children.content:
Tueely is a restaurant technology company that builds AI-powered menu tools for restaurants, cafes, and food service businesses. Its mission is to elevate restaurant menus with AI, making dining more accessible and guest experiences smoother.

Tueely offers two core products:
- MenuQR: Gives guests instant access to your menu through branded, scannable QR codes placed at every table.
- MenuGPT: Adds a conversational AI layer so guests can ask natural questions about ingredients, allergens, spice levels, and dish pairings before they order.

Tueely is designed to work as a non-disruptive add-on. It does not replace your existing POS system or payment flow — it layers on top of your current workflow to improve how guests discover and understand your menu.
children.section: About Tueely/Company Overview/What Is Tueely

children.title: Who Tueely Is For
children.summary: Target audience: restaurants, cafes, food halls, quick service, and any food business that uses physical or digital menus.
children.content:
Tueely is built for restaurants, cafes, quick service outlets, food halls, ghost kitchens, and any food business that wants to improve the guest menu experience.

It is particularly well suited for:
- High-volume restaurants where staff are stretched thin and guests need menu clarity fast
- Restaurants with complex menus featuring many modifiers, dietary notes, or allergy information
- Multi-location restaurant groups that want consistent menu access across all sites
- Owners and operators who want actionable insight about what guests look at and ask most
- Teams that care about accessibility — ensuring every guest can understand the menu regardless of language barrier or dietary complexity

Tueely does not require technical expertise to set up. Once menu data is shared, the Tueely team handles configuration so your staff can focus on service.
children.section: About Tueely/Company Overview/Who Tueely Is For

children.title: Getting Started with Tueely
children.summary: How to begin with Tueely: share your menu, get a guided walkthrough, and launch with your team.
children.content:
Getting started with Tueely is a simple three-step process:

1. Share your current menu — Send your existing PDF, document, or menu link. The Tueely team maps your real menu structure first, so the AI knows your actual items, ingredients, and service flow.

2. Get a guided walkthrough — A personalized demo shows MenuQR and MenuGPT working with realistic guest questions and your specific menu. This is not a generic product tour; it reflects your menu style and service model.

3. Launch with your team — Roll out in stages with your own branding while keeping your current POS process untouched. Staff training is minimal because Tueely does not change how your team takes orders.

To book a walkthrough, visit tueely.com/demo or contact the team directly.
children.section: About Tueely/Company Overview/Getting Started with Tueely

children.title: Tueely Products
children.summary: Overview of Tueely's two products: MenuQR for instant menu access via QR codes and MenuGPT for conversational AI menu questions.

children.title: MenuQR Overview
children.summary: MenuQR gives guests instant menu access through branded QR codes at every table.
children.content:
MenuQR is Tueely's QR code menu product. It replaces or supplements printed menus with clean, scannable QR codes that guests can use on any smartphone or tablet without installing an app.

Key features of MenuQR:
- Branded QR codes that match your restaurant's visual identity
- Instant access — no app download required; works in any mobile browser
- Real-time menu updates: change items, prices, or availability in minutes and every QR touchpoint reflects the update automatically
- Scannable from table tents, printed cards, wall signage, or receipt footers
- Works on iPhone, Android, and tablets across all modern browsers
- Tracks which items guests scan most so you can improve menu design and highlight popular items

MenuQR is the entry point for the Tueely guest experience. Once a guest scans the code, they can browse the menu and, if MenuGPT is enabled, ask questions about any item.
children.section: About Tueely/Tueely Products/MenuQR Overview

children.title: MenuGPT Overview
children.summary: MenuGPT is a conversational AI layer that answers guest questions about your menu including ingredients, allergens, spice levels, and dish pairings.
children.content:
MenuGPT is Tueely's AI-powered conversational menu assistant. Once a guest opens the menu through MenuQR, they can ask natural language questions about any item on the menu.

MenuGPT can answer questions like:
- 'Does the pasta contain gluten?'
- 'What is the spice level on the chicken curry?'
- 'Which dishes are vegan-friendly?'
- 'What goes well with the lamb chops?'
- 'Is the mushroom risotto nut-free?'

MenuGPT is trained on your specific menu data, so answers are accurate for your restaurant — not generic guesses. When you update your menu, MenuGPT updates too.

Benefits:
- Reduces the load on front-of-house staff for repetitive menu questions
- Increases guest confidence before ordering, reducing order errors and returns
- Helps guests with dietary restrictions feel catered to without asking staff
- Captures question data so you can see which items or dietary concerns come up most frequently
children.section: About Tueely/Tueely Products/MenuGPT Overview

children.title: MenuQR vs MenuGPT Differences
children.summary: Clarifying the distinction between MenuQR (QR access layer) and MenuGPT (conversational AI layer) and how they work together.
children.content:
MenuQR and MenuGPT are complementary products that work together but solve different problems:

MenuQR:
- Solves the access problem: How do guests quickly and cleanly view your menu?
- Provides a branded, scannable QR code that opens your menu instantly on any device
- No app installation needed; works in the browser
- Powers the initial menu browsing experience

MenuGPT:
- Solves the understanding problem: How do guests get answers to menu questions without bothering staff?
- Adds an AI chat layer on top of the MenuQR experience
- Answers specific questions about ingredients, allergens, preparations, and pairings
- Trained specifically on your menu data for accurate, restaurant-specific answers

Together they form a complete menu discovery experience: guests scan, browse, and ask — all from their phone, without needing a staff member to explain the menu.
children.section: About Tueely/Tueely Products/MenuQR vs MenuGPT Differences

title: Setup and Integration
summary: How to set up Tueely, integrate it with existing systems, configure menu data, deploy QR codes, and onboard your team.

children.title: Menu Data and Configuration
children.summary: How to share your menu with Tueely, what formats are accepted, and how menu updates work.

children.title: Accepted Menu Formats
children.summary: Tueely accepts PDF menus, Word documents, Google Docs, spreadsheet files, and existing menu links.
children.content:
To set up Tueely, you share your current menu in any common format. Tueely accepts:
- PDF menus: The most common format. Simply send your existing printed menu as a PDF.
- Word or Google Docs: If you keep your menu in a document, share it directly.
- Spreadsheet files (CSV or Excel): Useful for restaurants with structured menu data including items, prices, and modifiers.
- Existing menu links: If you have an online menu (e.g., on your restaurant website or a third-party platform), share the URL.
- Photos of physical menus: In some cases the team can work from high-quality menu photos.

The Tueely team reviews your menu data and structures it in the system, including item names, descriptions, ingredients, allergen tags, and any modifiers. You do not need to reformat your menu — share it as-is.
children.section: Setup and Integration/Menu Data and Configuration/Accepted Menu Formats

children.title: Updating Your Menu
children.summary: How to update menu items, prices, and availability after going live with Tueely.
children.content:
Keeping your Tueely menu up to date is designed to be fast and easy:

- Item updates: Add, remove, or modify menu items through the Tueely dashboard or by sending updated menu files to the support team.
- Price changes: Update prices directly in the dashboard and they reflect on guest-facing QR menus immediately.
- Availability toggles: Mark items as unavailable (e.g., 'sold out today') without removing them from the full menu.
- Seasonal menus: Switch between saved menu versions for seasonal changes, holiday specials, or timed promotions.

All updates propagate instantly to every QR code linked to your account. There is no need to reprint QR codes when your menu changes — this is one of the key advantages over static printed menus. MenuGPT also updates automatically when your menu data changes, so AI answers stay accurate after every update.
children.section: Setup and Integration/Menu Data and Configuration/Updating Your Menu

children.title: Menu Structure and Categories
children.summary: How Tueely organizes your menu into categories, subcategories, and items for the best guest experience.
children.content:
Tueely structures your menu into a hierarchy that mirrors how guests think about food:

- Categories: Top-level groupings such as Starters, Mains, Desserts, Drinks, or any custom sections you prefer.
- Subcategories: Optional groupings within a category — for example, Mains → Meat Dishes, Vegetarian, Pasta.
- Items: Individual dishes with names, descriptions, prices, ingredients, and modifiers.
- Tags: Dietary and allergen tags such as Vegan, Gluten-Free, Contains Nuts, Dairy-Free that appear alongside items.

The team configures the structure to match your actual menu layout. You can request changes to categories or hierarchy at any time through the dashboard or support channel. A well-structured menu leads to better MenuGPT answers and faster guest browsing with MenuQR.
children.section: Setup and Integration/Menu Data and Configuration/Menu Structure and Categories

children.title: QR Code Deployment
children.summary: How to print and deploy MenuQR codes in your restaurant, placement best practices, and customization options.

children.title: Generating and Printing QR Codes
children.summary: How to generate branded QR codes from the Tueely dashboard and best practices for printing and placing them.
children.content:
Once your menu is configured in Tueely, you can generate your QR codes from the dashboard:

1. Go to the QR Codes section in your Tueely dashboard.
2. Select the menu or menu section you want the QR code to link to.
3. Download the QR code as a PNG or SVG file in your preferred size.
4. Apply your branding (logo, brand colors, call-to-action text like 'Scan to view menu') using the built-in customization options or export for use in your own design files.
5. Print on table tents, menu cards, wall signage, or any surface guests will see.

Best practices for printing:
- Use a minimum of 2.5 cm x 2.5 cm (1 inch x 1 inch) QR code size for reliable scanning
- Ensure high contrast between the QR code and its background
- Laminate table tents for durability in high-spill environments
- Place at sitting eye level or slightly above for natural scanning posture
children.section: Setup and Integration/QR Code Deployment/Generating and Printing QR Codes

children.title: QR Code Placement Best Practices
children.summary: Where to place MenuQR codes for maximum guest engagement at table, counter, and takeaway formats.
children.content:
Where you place your QR codes significantly affects how many guests actually use them:

Table service restaurants:
- Table tents at the center of each table are the most effective placement
- QR codes on the physical menu cover or header for hybrid printed + digital experience
- Placement near condiment holders so guests have something to scan while waiting

Counter and quick service:
- QR codes at the ordering counter at eye level
- Digital display boards with QR code insets for pre-order browsing while queuing
- QR codes on takeaway bags or packaging for reorder and repeat visits

Bar and lounge areas:
- Coaster-style QR cards for drink menus
- Bar ledge card holders for cocktail and food menus

Multiple QR codes can point to the same menu or different sections (e.g., a drinks-only menu at the bar vs. the full food menu at tables). All can be managed from the same Tueely dashboard.
children.section: Setup and Integration/QR Code Deployment/QR Code Placement Best Practices

children.title: QR Code Branding and Customization
children.summary: How to customize QR code appearance with your restaurant brand colors, logo, and call-to-action text.
children.content:
Tueely's QR codes are fully brandable to match your restaurant's visual identity:

- Logo integration: Add your restaurant logo to the center of the QR code
- Brand colors: Change the QR code foreground color to match your brand while maintaining scan reliability
- Background color: Customize the background to match your printed materials
- Call-to-action text: Add text around the QR code such as 'Scan to view menu', 'Ask MenuGPT anything', or 'View today's specials'
- Shape styles: Round, square, or dot-style QR modules depending on your design preference

All customization is available from the Tueely dashboard QR Designer. Exported files are available in PNG, SVG, and PDF formats suitable for both digital use and professional print production. If you have a specific brand guideline requirement, the Tueely design team can assist with custom QR artwork.
children.section: Setup and Integration/QR Code Deployment/QR Code Branding and Customization

children.title: POS and System Integration
children.summary: How Tueely works alongside your existing POS, payment flow, and other restaurant management systems.

children.title: POS Compatibility and Non-Disruption
children.summary: Tueely does not replace your POS. It works as a menu discovery layer on top of your existing ordering and payment flow.
children.content:
Tueely is designed to be a non-disruptive add-on to your restaurant operation. It does not replace your POS (Point of Sale) system, payment processor, or existing ordering flow.

How this works in practice:
- Guests use Tueely (MenuQR + MenuGPT) to browse the menu and understand options
- Ordering still happens exactly as it does today: verbally with staff, via your existing digital ordering system, or at the counter
- Payment is handled by your existing POS and payment setup — Tueely has no involvement in transactions
- There is no integration required with your POS to go live; Tueely operates independently as a menu layer

This means:
- Zero risk to your order accuracy or payment flow
- No retraining your team on a new POS or workflow
- No downtime risk from a new system deployment
- You can evaluate Tueely's guest impact without committing to any system change

For restaurants who want to explore deeper integrations (e.g., syncing menu availability from their POS directly into Tueely), that is available as a custom integration discussed with the Tueely team.
children.section: Setup and Integration/POS and System Integration/POS Compatibility and Non-Disruption

children.title: Multi-Location Setup
children.summary: How restaurant groups with multiple locations manage separate menus and QR codes from a single Tueely account.
children.content:
Tueely supports restaurant groups operating multiple locations from a single account:

- Each location gets its own menu configuration, QR codes, and analytics within the same account
- Menus can be shared across locations (for chains with identical menus) or kept separate (for locations with different offerings)
- QR codes are location-specific — scanning a QR at Location A always opens Location A's menu
- Dashboard filtering lets you view analytics per location or across all locations combined
- Team access can be scoped per location so individual location managers only manage their own data

For franchise or licensing models, the Tueely team can set up account structures to match your operational hierarchy. Contact the team at tueely.com/demo to discuss multi-location rollout planning.
children.section: Setup and Integration/POS and System Integration/Multi-Location Setup

title: Features and Capabilities
summary: Detailed features of Tueely including menu insights, allergen tagging, multilingual support, team updates, and guest interaction analytics.

children.title: Guest Experience Features
children.summary: Features that directly improve the guest experience: instant menu access, AI Q&A, dietary filtering, and cross-device compatibility.

children.title: Device Compatibility and Accessibility
children.summary: Tueely works on iPhone, Android, and tablets without requiring app installation, using any modern browser.
children.content:
Tueely is built mobile-first and works across all major devices and browsers:

- iPhone and iPad: Works in Safari, Chrome, or any browser — no App Store download needed
- Android phones and tablets: Works in Chrome, Firefox, Samsung Browser, or any modern browser
- Desktop: Accessible on laptop and desktop browsers for guests at high-top tables or counter seating
- No app installation: The entire guest experience runs in the browser. Guests scan the QR code, the menu opens immediately.

Accessibility considerations:
- Text sizing is optimized for mobile reading without zooming
- High-contrast mode support for visually impaired guests
- Screen reader compatible layout for guests using assistive technology
- Language support: MenuGPT can respond in the guest's language if the menu supports multilingual data

Tueely does not collect guest personal information during the menu browsing or MenuGPT interaction unless the guest explicitly submits a feedback form.
children.section: Features and Capabilities/Guest Experience Features/Device Compatibility and Accessibility

children.title: Allergen and Dietary Information
children.summary: How Tueely handles allergen tagging, dietary filters, and accurate responses to allergy-related questions via MenuGPT.
children.content:
Allergen and dietary accuracy is one of the most important capabilities in Tueely:

Allergen tagging:
- Each menu item can be tagged with allergens based on the 14 major allergens: gluten, crustaceans, eggs, fish, peanuts, soybeans, milk/dairy, nuts, celery, mustard, sesame, sulphites, lupin, and molluscs.
- Tags are visible alongside items in the MenuQR display
- MenuGPT uses these tags to accurately answer questions like 'Does this dish contain peanuts?' or 'Which pizzas are dairy-free?'

Dietary filters:
- Guests can filter the menu by dietary preference: Vegan, Vegetarian, Gluten-Free, Halal, Kosher, Nut-Free
- Filters update the visible menu in real time so only matching items are shown

Accuracy responsibility:
- Allergen data is only as accurate as the information provided by the restaurant
- Tueely displays a standard advisory note reminding guests that while allergen information is maintained carefully, kitchen cross-contamination risks should always be verified with staff for severe allergies
- Restaurants should update allergen data immediately when suppliers or recipes change
children.section: Features and Capabilities/Guest Experience Features/Allergen and Dietary Information

children.title: MenuGPT Conversation Capabilities
children.summary: What types of questions MenuGPT can handle and its limitations.
children.content:
MenuGPT is trained on your specific menu data and can handle a wide range of guest questions:

MenuGPT can answer:
- Ingredient questions: 'What is in the Caesar salad dressing?'
- Allergen questions: 'Is the fish and chips gluten-free?'
- Dietary questions: 'Do you have vegan options for dessert?'
- Comparison questions: 'What is the difference between the two steak options?'
- Recommendation questions: 'What would you recommend for someone who likes spicy food?'
- Pairing questions: 'What wine pairs well with the lamb?'
- Spice level questions: 'How spicy is the vindaloo on a scale of 1 to 10?'
- Size/portion questions: 'How large is the sharing platter?'
- Preparation questions: 'Is the salmon served raw or cooked?'

MenuGPT limitations:
- It can only answer questions based on the menu data you have provided; it does not know information not in your menu
- It cannot process orders or add items to a cart
- It cannot access real-time inventory or sold-out items unless availability toggles are kept up to date
- For severe allergen concerns, MenuGPT always advises guests to confirm with staff due to cross-contamination risk
children.section: Features and Capabilities/Guest Experience Features/MenuGPT Conversation Capabilities

children.title: Restaurant Team Features
children.summary: Features built for restaurant owners, managers, and staff: menu management dashboard, team access roles, and analytics.

children.title: Tueely Dashboard Overview
children.summary: The Tueely dashboard lets owners and managers manage menus, generate QR codes, view analytics, and control team access.
children.content:
The Tueely dashboard is the control center for everything in your account:

Menu Management:
- Add, edit, or remove items, categories, prices, and descriptions
- Toggle item availability without deleting items
- Upload or link your latest menu version
- Preview how your menu looks to guests before publishing

QR Code Management:
- Generate new QR codes for different tables, areas, or menus
- Download QR codes in print-ready formats
- View scan count per QR code to identify high-traffic tables

Analytics:
- Daily and weekly scan counts per location or QR code
- Most viewed menu items and categories
- Most asked question topics via MenuGPT
- Guest filter usage (e.g., how often guests filter for 'Vegan')

Team Access:
- Invite team members with specific roles: Admin (full access), Manager (menu + analytics), Staff (view only)
- Multi-location operators can scope access per site

Support:
- In-dashboard chat support and documentation links
children.section: Features and Capabilities/Restaurant Team Features/Tueely Dashboard Overview

children.title: Analytics and Guest Insights
children.summary: How Tueely's analytics help restaurants understand what guests scan, ask, and care about most.
children.content:
Tueely captures anonymized interaction data that gives restaurants actionable menu intelligence:

Scan Analytics:
- Total QR code scans per day, week, or month
- Peak scanning times to understand when guests engage with menus most
- Per-table or per-QR scan breakdown to identify table utilization patterns

Menu Engagement:
- Most-viewed items and categories
- Items with low engagement (candidates for better descriptions or promotion)
- Filter usage: which dietary filters guests use most (e.g., Vegan being used heavily suggests adding more vegan labels or options)

MenuGPT Insights:
- Question categories most asked by guests (ingredients, allergens, recommendations)
- Specific items that receive the most AI questions (may indicate unclear descriptions in base menu)
- Unanswered question patterns (topics not covered by your current menu data — useful for adding missing information)

All analytics data is anonymized. No guest personal data is collected or stored. Analytics are available in the dashboard and can be exported as CSV for use in your own reports or restaurant management tools.
children.section: Features and Capabilities/Restaurant Team Features/Analytics and Guest Insights

children.title: Team Roles and Permissions
children.summary: The three access roles in Tueely: Admin, Manager, and Staff — what each can do.
children.content:
Tueely provides three access roles to control what each team member can do in the dashboard:

Admin:
- Full account access including billing and subscription management
- Can invite, edit, and remove team members
- Can create and delete QR codes and menus
- Access to all analytics across all locations
- Can contact Tueely support and manage account settings

Manager:
- Can update menu items, descriptions, prices, and availability
- Can generate and download QR codes
- Can view analytics for assigned locations
- Cannot manage billing or team membership

Staff:
- View-only access to menus
- Useful for front-of-house staff who need to reference the digital menu internally
- Cannot edit menu data, manage QR codes, or view analytics

Role assignments are made by the Admin and can be updated at any time. For multi-location operators, role access can be scoped to specific locations so a manager at one site cannot edit menus for another site.
children.section: Features and Capabilities/Restaurant Team Features/Team Roles and Permissions

title: Pricing and Plans
summary: Tueely pricing tiers, what is included in each plan, trial options, billing, and how to upgrade or cancel.

children.title: Pricing Overview
children.summary: Tueely's pricing structure, plan tiers, and what each includes.

children.title: Plan Tiers and What Is Included
children.summary: Tueely offers a Starter plan with MenuQR and a Growth plan adding MenuGPT, analytics, and multi-location support.
children.content:
Tueely offers tiered pricing to match the needs of restaurants at different stages:

Starter Plan:
- MenuQR: Unlimited QR code generation for your venue
- Up to 1 menu location
- Basic menu management dashboard
- Standard branding on QR codes
- Email support
- Best for: Smaller restaurants testing digital menus for the first time

Growth Plan:
- Everything in Starter plus:
- MenuGPT: AI-powered conversational menu Q&A
- Advanced analytics including scan tracking and MenuGPT question insights
- Up to 5 menu locations
- Full QR code branding customization
- Dietary filter and allergen tagging tools
- Priority support
- Best for: Growing restaurants that want to reduce staff menu load and gain guest insights

Enterprise Plan:
- Everything in Growth plus:
- Unlimited locations
- Dedicated account manager
- Custom POS integration options
- SLA-backed support with guaranteed response times
- White-label options for franchise or licensing models
- Custom onboarding and staff training
- Best for: Multi-site restaurant groups, franchises, and hospitality groups

For current pricing, visit tueely.com or book a demo at tueely.com/demo.
children.section: Pricing and Plans/Pricing Overview/Plan Tiers and What Is Included

children.title: Trial and Free Demo
children.summary: Tueely offers a guided demo and trial period before committing to a paid plan.
children.content:
You can evaluate Tueely before committing to a subscription:

Free Demo:
- Book a personalized walkthrough at tueely.com/demo
- The demo uses your actual menu data so you see exactly how MenuQR and MenuGPT would work for your restaurant
- No credit card required to book a demo
- A Tueely team member walks you through the full setup and answers your questions

Trial Period:
- After the demo, a trial period may be available for qualifying restaurants
- During the trial, you deploy Tueely in a live or limited section of your restaurant
- All features of the plan you are trialing are available during the trial period
- At the end of the trial, you choose to subscribe or the account is paused with no charge

Contact the Tueely team via tueely.com/demo to start the process.
children.section: Pricing and Plans/Pricing Overview/Trial and Free Demo

children.title: Billing and Cancellation
children.summary: How billing works for Tueely subscriptions, monthly vs annual options, and how to cancel.
children.content:
Tueely subscriptions are billed on a monthly or annual basis:

Billing:
- Monthly billing: Pay month-to-month with no long-term commitment
- Annual billing: Pay for a full year upfront at a discounted rate vs. monthly equivalent
- Billing is managed through Paddle, a trusted payment provider
- Invoices are sent by email and accessible in the dashboard under Account > Billing
- Accepted payment methods: major credit and debit cards, and select digital payment methods

Upgrading:
- Upgrade from Starter to Growth or Growth to Enterprise at any time from Account > Billing
- Upgrade takes effect immediately; you are charged a pro-rated amount for the remainder of the current billing period

Cancelling:
- Cancel at any time from Account > Billing > Cancel Subscription
- Cancellation takes effect at the end of your current billing period; you retain access until then
- No refunds are issued for unused time on monthly plans; annual plan refund terms are in the Refund Policy at tueely.com/refund-policy

For billing questions contact the Tueely team through the dashboard support channel.
children.section: Pricing and Plans/Pricing Overview/Billing and Cancellation

title: Support and Troubleshooting
summary: How to get help with Tueely, common troubleshooting steps for QR codes and MenuGPT, and contact methods.

children.title: Getting Help
children.summary: How to contact Tueely support, self-serve resources, and expected response times.

children.title: Support Channels and Contact
children.summary: Tueely support is available through in-dashboard chat, email, and LinkedIn.
children.content:
Tueely offers multiple ways to get help:

In-dashboard support:
- Use the chat widget inside the Tueely dashboard for the fastest response
- Available for all plan tiers
- Typical response time: within a few hours during business hours

Email support:
- Send questions to the support email listed in your dashboard under Account > Support
- Best for non-urgent technical or billing questions
- Include your account name and a description of the issue for faster resolution

Demo and sales inquiries:
- Book a demo or reach the sales team at tueely.com/demo

Community and social:
- Follow Tueely on LinkedIn at linkedin.com/company/tueely for updates, tips, and case studies
- GitHub: github.com/tueely for open-source components

Priority support is available on the Growth and Enterprise plans with faster response time guarantees. Enterprise customers have a dedicated account manager as their primary point of contact.
children.section: Support and Troubleshooting/Getting Help/Support Channels and Contact

children.title: QR Code Not Scanning Troubleshooting
children.summary: Steps to fix QR codes that are not scanning correctly — print quality, size, contrast, and lighting issues.
children.content:
If guests report that a QR code is not scanning, work through these checks:

1. QR code size: Ensure the printed QR code is at least 2.5 cm x 2.5 cm (1 inch x 1 inch). Smaller codes are harder for phone cameras to read.

2. Print quality: Low-resolution printing causes scanning failures. Always print from SVG or high-resolution PNG source files, not compressed images from Word documents or screenshots.

3. Contrast and color: There must be strong contrast between the dark QR modules and the light background. Avoid printing dark QR codes on dark materials or light codes on light materials.

4. Damage and glare: Torn, scratched, or laminated QR codes with excessive glare may not scan reliably. Replace damaged codes and position laminated cards to reduce direct light reflection.

5. Distance: Most phone cameras scan QR codes best from 15–40 cm (6–16 inches) away. If the code is too close or too far, it may not trigger.

6. Browser camera permissions: If guests are scanning using a browser-based QR scanner, ensure the browser has camera permission granted on their device.

If the QR code itself appears correct but links to the wrong menu or an error page, log in to the Tueely dashboard, navigate to QR Codes, and verify the code is linked to the correct active menu.
children.section: Support and Troubleshooting/Getting Help/QR Code Not Scanning Troubleshooting

children.title: MenuGPT Giving Incorrect Answers
children.summary: What to do when MenuGPT gives inaccurate or outdated answers about your menu.
children.content:
If MenuGPT is giving incorrect or outdated answers, the most common causes and solutions are:

1. Outdated menu data: MenuGPT answers are based on the menu data loaded in your Tueely account. If you have updated prices, changed ingredients, or removed items but have not updated your Tueely menu, answers may be wrong. Solution: Update your menu in the dashboard or send the updated menu file to the support team.

2. Missing information: If MenuGPT says it does not know the answer to a question about a real menu item, that item likely does not have enough detail in its description. Solution: Add ingredient lists, allergen tags, and more descriptive content to the relevant items in your dashboard.

3. Vague item descriptions: Short or generic descriptions like 'Chef's special pasta' without any ingredients will produce vague MenuGPT answers. Solution: Add meaningful descriptions including key ingredients and preparation style.

4. Allergen tags not applied: If guests report that MenuGPT answered incorrectly about allergens, check that all allergen tags are applied accurately in the dashboard item editor.

5. AI interpretation edge cases: Occasionally MenuGPT may misinterpret an ambiguous question. If a specific question consistently produces wrong answers, report it through the support channel with the exact question text so the team can investigate.
children.section: Support and Troubleshooting/Getting Help/MenuGPT Giving Incorrect Answers

children.title: Frequently Asked Questions
children.summary: Common questions from restaurant owners and managers about Tueely, MenuQR, and MenuGPT.

children.title: FAQs About Setup and Going Live
children.summary: Common questions about how long setup takes, what is required from the restaurant, and how to go live.
children.content:
Frequently asked questions about setup:

Q: How long does setup take?
A: Most restaurants can be live within a few days of sharing their menu data. The Tueely team handles the configuration. Complex menus with many modifiers or dietary tags may take a few extra days. Enterprise onboarding timelines are discussed individually.

Q: Do I need a website or existing online menu?
A: No. Tueely works from your existing menu file. Share a PDF, a photo, or a spreadsheet and the team will set it up.

Q: Can I do a partial rollout before going fully live?
A: Yes. Many restaurants start with one section (e.g., one dining area or the drinks menu) and expand after confirming the flow works for their team.

Q: Does my POS provider need to approve or be involved?
A: No. Tueely is independent of your POS. No POS integration is required for standard setup.

Q: Will going live with Tueely slow down my service?
A: No. Tueely is an add-on that guests use independently. It does not add steps to your ordering or payment process — it replaces or supplements the 'guest reads printed menu' step.
children.section: Support and Troubleshooting/Frequently Asked Questions/FAQs About Setup and Going Live

children.title: FAQs About MenuGPT and AI
children.summary: Common questions about how MenuGPT works, its accuracy, data privacy, and what it cannot do.
children.content:
Frequently asked questions about MenuGPT:

Q: Is MenuGPT the same as ChatGPT?
A: MenuGPT is powered by AI but is specifically trained on your restaurant's menu data. It is not a general-purpose AI like ChatGPT — it only answers questions about your specific menu.

Q: How accurate is MenuGPT?
A: Accuracy depends on the quality of your menu data. The more complete your item descriptions, ingredient lists, and allergen tags, the more accurate MenuGPT will be. Vague descriptions produce vague answers.

Q: Does MenuGPT store guest conversations?
A: Guest conversations are used anonymously to improve question insights in your analytics dashboard. No personal guest data is stored or linked to individual guests.

Q: Can MenuGPT handle multiple languages?
A: MenuGPT can respond in the guest's language for questions asked in common languages, even if your source menu is in English. Full multilingual menu data support is available on request.

Q: Can guests order through MenuGPT?
A: No. MenuGPT is a menu information assistant only. It cannot take orders, add items to a cart, or process payments. Ordering continues through your existing staff or ordering system.
children.section: Support and Troubleshooting/Frequently Asked Questions/FAQs About MenuGPT and AI

children.title: FAQs About Billing and Account
children.summary: Common questions about billing, cancellation, refunds, and account management.
children.content:
Frequently asked questions about billing and account management:

Q: Can I cancel anytime?
A: Yes. Cancel at any time from Account > Billing > Cancel Subscription. Access continues until the end of the current billing period.

Q: Is there a free trial?
A: A trial period is available after the demo for qualifying restaurants. Contact the team at tueely.com/demo to discuss terms.

Q: What happens to my menu data if I cancel?
A: Your menu data is retained for 60 days after cancellation so you can reactivate easily if needed. After 60 days it is deleted per the Tueely data retention policy.

Q: Can I change plans mid-month?
A: Yes. Upgrades take effect immediately with pro-rated billing. Downgrades take effect at the start of the next billing period.

Q: Who handles payment processing?
A: Payments are processed through Paddle, a trusted global payment provider. Paddle handles all payment security and compliance including PCI DSS.

Q: I was charged incorrectly — what should I do?
A: Contact support through the dashboard or email. Include your invoice number and a description of the discrepancy. The team aims to resolve billing issues within one business day.
children.section: Support and Troubleshooting/Frequently Asked Questions/FAQs About Billing and Account
